title: lycianthes parasitica var. angatii
intro: Lycianthes parasitica var. angatii, also known as the Philippine Lycianthes, is a plant species native to the Philippines. It is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, and has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Lycianthes parasitica var. angatii can be made into infusions, decoctions, or topical creams, and have been used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and skin infections.
